actions.title: Add fallback metadata to index.html
actions.description: Add missing metadata in the framework's server-visible metadata surface.
actions.frameworkStrategy: Update index.html for crawler-visible fallback metadata; route-specific metadata may need prerendering or SSR.

futureAutomation.reason: Metadata changes affect production copy and crawler/share-preview signals, so generated changes should be reviewed.

actions.title: Add JSON-LD with review
actions.description: Add or fix conservative structured data only after confirming schema type and business facts.
actions.frameworkStrategy: Add static JSON-LD only when it accurately describes the app shell.

futureAutomation.reason: Structured data must be factually accurate; ShipReady should not invent schema facts without review.

actions.title: Review page heading structure
actions.description: The page should have one clear H1.
actions.frameworkStrategy: Locate the relevant page or component and make semantic/content changes with review.

futureAutomation.reason: Content and accessibility text changes require human review for accuracy.

actions.title: Update sitemap.xml
actions.description: Replace the invalid sitemap response with a valid sitemap.
actions.frameworkStrategy: Add a public sitemap.xml file using the framework's static asset convention. Use audited URL https://mon-guide-fodmap.com/.

futureAutomation.reason: The URL set or lower-confidence repo detection should be reviewed before generating sitemap changes.

optionalNotes: Theme color is not set: Optional theme-color metadata was not found in the initial HTML.
limitations: This is a read-only plan. No files were modified and no patches were generated.
Future automation must re-check the repo before writing changes.
File writing is disabled; use `shipready fix --dry-run` for supported patch previews.
This command is read-only and does not modify repository files.
Route-specific metadata in a Vite SPA may only appear after JavaScript rendering.
Future fixes may need to recommend prerendering or SSG instead of relying on JS-injected metadata for crawler visibility.
